evals.name: vague-perf-request
evals.prompt: our API endpoints are getting slow, especially the dashboard ones — can you help me figure out what to do about it?
evals.expected_output: Should classify intent, score ambiguity across 4 dimensions (all High), and ask a targeted first question via AskUserQuestion. Should NOT propose specific solutions yet.

assertions.text: Classifies intent into one of the defined types (Refactoring, Build, Mid-sized, etc.)
assertions.description: Response should explicitly state the request type classification and confidence level

assertions.text: Scores ambiguity across dimensions (Scope, Acceptance, Approach, Boundaries)
assertions.description: Should present ambiguity scores for the 4 dimensions, with Scope and Acceptance rated High

assertions.text: Asks exactly one targeted question, not multiple questions at once
assertions.description: Should ask a single focused question about the highest-ambiguity dimension, not dump a list of questions

assertions.text: Does not propose specific technical solutions
assertions.description: Should not recommend caching, query optimization, CDN, or any concrete fix — that comes after the interview

evals.name: multi-system-notification
evals.prompt: we want to add a notification system — the PM mentioned email, push, and maybe Slack integration but hasn't specified much else
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Build, identify high ambiguity in Scope and Boundaries, and ask about MVP scope vs full vision.

assertions.description: Multi-channel notification system is a new feature build, should be classified as Build
Three channels mentioned with 'maybe' qualifier — unclear what's in scope vs out of scope

assertions.text: First question targets scope boundaries or MVP definition
assertions.description: With multiple channels and vague PM requirements, the biggest unknown is what to build first

assertions.text: Offers multi-choice options rather than open-ended question
assertions.description: Skill instructs to prefer multi-choice when possible — e.g. 'Which channels for MVP: (A) email only, (B) email + push, (C) all three'

evals.name: architecture-exploration
evals.prompt: we're thinking about splitting our Django monolith into services but honestly I'm not sure if it's the right move or where to even start — help me think through this?
evals.expected_output: Should classify as Architecture/Collaborative, score Approach and Acceptance as High, and explore constraints before any recommendation.

assertions.text: Classifies as Architecture or Collaborative type
assertions.description: User explicitly says 'help me think through' (Collaborative signal) about system design (Architecture signal)
Multiple valid approaches exist — microservices, modular monolith, partial extraction — and user is unsure

assertions.text: Does not recommend microservices or any specific architecture
assertions.description: Brainstorm gathers requirements, it does not propose solutions. No 'you should use X' before understanding constraints

assertions.text: First question explores constraints (team size, pain points, or scale)
assertions.description: Architecture decisions depend heavily on constraints — the first question should surface what's driving the split
